title: Else & Else If 🔀
description: Create multiple paths and backup plans for your code to follow.

slides.title: The Backup Plan 🛡️
If/Else in Action 💻
slides.content: Try changing the password to 'secret123' and see how the path changes:

quizData.question: Does the 'else' keyword require a condition inside parentheses ()?
quizData.explanation: No! 'else' acts as a universal backup plan. It simply catches anything that the preceding 'if' didn't catch, so it doesn't need its own test.

slides.title: Multiple Paths: Else If 🛤️
Testing Else If 💻
slides.content: Change the hour variable to test different times of the day:

quizData.question: If you have an if / else if / else chain, how many of those blocks can possibly run?
quizData.explanation: JavaScript guarantees that EXACTLY ONE block in an if/else chain will run. As soon as it finds a truthy condition, it executes that block and ignores the rest.

slides.title: Order Matters! ⚠️

quizData.question: Which structure should always be the VERY LAST thing in an if/else chain?
quizData.explanation: The 'else' statement acts as the final catch-all. It must always be at the bottom of the chain to handle any cases the previous checks missed.
